llm_explanation:
- Bank inherits ReentrancyGuard, which (as in common implementations) stores a state variable in storage slot 0. Therefore, the mapping credits is not at slot 0 but at a subsequent slot (typically slot 1).
- The test computes the user’s credits storage location with slot 0: bytes32 user_credits_slot = keccak256(abi.encode(user, credits_slot)); where credits_slot is hardcoded to 0. This is incorrect. vm.load(address(bank), keccak256(abi.encode(user, 0))) will always read from an uninitialized slot (i.e., zero), not the actual credits mapping.
- Consequently, user_creditsBefore is always 0. The assertion assertGt(user_creditsBefore, type(uint256).max - msg_value) can never hold for any valid msg_value ∈ [0, 2^256-1], since 0 > (type(uint256).max - msg_value) is always false.
- Even disregarding the slot mistake, deposit uses checked arithmetic in Solidity >=0.8 (credits[msg.sender] += msg.value), so any true overflow condition would revert. Moreover, with the constraint that only up to 10 billion ETH can be put in circulation via vm.deal, it is impossible to raise credits near 2^256-1 to satisfy the overflow precondition anyway.

The claim “deposit never reverts (internal or external)” is false. While deposit has no explicit require checks, it is protected by nonReentrant. During a withdraw, when the Bank sends ETH to a contract, that contract’s receive can attempt a reentrant call to bank.deposit(), which will revert due to ReentrancyGuard. The provided test records all call frames and flags this reverted internal call to deposit, satisfying hasDepositReverted(accesses).

Bank.deposit has no require/assert and performs only a single SSTORE on credits[msg.sender] plus accepting msg.value. The only practical way for deposit to fail is an EVM-level error such as out-of-gas. However, the specification’s hasDepositReverted() only flags frames where acc.kind == 0 (Call) and acc.reverted is true. In practice with vm.stopAndReturnStateDiff, out-of-gas on a call to deposit is not surfaced as acc.reverted = true in the returned AccountAccess; this is evidenced by the previous failing run where Bank::deposit ran OutOfGas yet the assertion did not see acc.reverted = true. Other avenues to force a true REVERT are unavailable:
- STATICCALL would trigger a state-change-during-static revert but would be recorded as kind = 3 (StaticCall), which the spec filters out (kind must be 0).
- Arithmetic overflow via credits[msg.sender] += msg.value is unreachable given credits start at 0, we cannot write Bank’s storage (only vm.deal/vm.prank allowed), and the ETH-in-circulation cap prevents overflowing to 2^256−1.
- Insufficient balance on the caller prevents the call from entering deposit and does not produce a reverted deposit frame that matches the spec’s predicate.
Therefore, no concretization of the abstract variables can make hasDepositReverted(accesses) return true, and the property as instrumented is valid.
